items.summary: Overhaul the CyberMentor response engine in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` to implement a full AI personality system acting as Teacher + Software Engineer + Research Assistant. Every response must follow this mandatory 7-part structure: (1) Quick Answer — a concise 1–3 sentence direct answer, (2) Deep Explanation — full structured body with headings and bullet points, (3) Real-World Example section, (4) Code Example section (when topic is code-related, using fenced code blocks with language tags), (5) Common Mistakes section, (6) Extra Tips / Pro Tips section, (7) Closing follow-up prompt ('Would you like me to go deeper on any part of this?'). Responses must never be short. Every response must open with a varied empathetic phrase chosen randomly from at least 15 options (e.g., 'Great question!', 'That's something many students find tricky — let me break it down for you.', 'You're asking exactly the right thing!'). Use first-person language ('I', 'we') and occasional encouragement ('You're on the right track!', 'Keep going — you're almost there!'). The initial page-load greeting must be randomly selected from a pool of at least 10 warm, human-like messages.

Implement automatic Hindi/English/Hinglish language detection in the CyberMentor response engine (`frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts`). When a user submits a question, detect whether the input contains Devanagari Unicode characters (Hindi script) or common romanized Hindi keywords (kya, kaise, batao, samjhao, mujhe, matlab, sikho, bhai, yaar, etc.). If Hindi or Hinglish is detected, the response must be written in Hindi or Hinglish accordingly. If English is detected, respond in English. Implement a `detectLanguage(input: string): 'hindi' | 'hinglish' | 'english'` utility function and apply it before generating each response.

Implement a Deep Research Mode in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` and `frontend/src/pages/CyberMentorPage.tsx`. Before generating a response, the engine must visibly simulate these internal reasoning steps shown as animated status lines: (1) 'Analyzing question...', (2) 'Breaking into sub-topics...', (3) 'Searching knowledge base...', (4) 'Thinking step by step...', (5) 'Generating structured answer...'. Add a toggle button labeled 'Deep Research Mode' in the CyberMentorPage UI. When enabled, these animated thinking steps are displayed sequentially before the response streams in. When disabled, responses stream immediately without the thinking animation. The deep research flag must be stored in React state.

Implement a Debug Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message contains error/debug pattern keywords (e.g., 'error', 'exception', 'TypeError', 'SyntaxError', 'undefined', 'null', 'cannot read', 'is not a function', 'failed', 'bug', 'not working', or contains a stack trace pattern), automatically activate debug mode. In debug mode, the response structure must change to: (1) Error Explanation — what the error means in plain English, (2) Root Cause Analysis — why this error occurs, (3) Step-by-Step Fix — numbered steps to resolve it, (4) Corrected Code — fixed code in a fenced code block, (5) Prevention Tips — how to avoid this error in future. Add a visible 'Debug Mode' badge/indicator on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

Implement a Project Generation Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message contains project-request keywords (e.g., 'build a project', 'create a project', 'make an app', 'project idea', 'generate project', 'full stack project', 'portfolio project', 'make project'), the response must follow this structure: (1) Project Overview — name, description, and tech stack, (2) Folder Structure — shown as a code block with directory tree, (3) Frontend Code — key component/page code snippets, (4) Backend API — endpoint definitions and handler code, (5) Database Schema — table/collection definitions, (6) README Instructions — setup and run steps, (7) Deployment Steps. Add a visible 'Project Generator' badge on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

Implement a PPT Notes Mode in the CyberMentor response engine. When a user's message requests notes, PPT, slides, or a summary (keywords: 'notes', 'ppt', 'slides', 'summarize', 'summary', 'bullet points', 'cheat sheet'), the response must be formatted as structured bullet-point notes ready for PPT export. Use clear headings for each section and concise bullet points under each heading. Add a visible 'PPT Notes Mode' badge on the assistant message bubble when this mode is active.

Implement a robust conversation memory system in `frontend/src/pages/CyberMentorPage.tsx`. Store the full conversation history in React state as an array of `{role: 'user' | 'assistant', content: string, timestamp: Date}` objects. Pass the last 10 messages as context to the response generation function so the mentor can reference previous questions (e.g., 'As I mentioned earlier...'). Display a message count indicator in the chat header. Add a 'Clear Chat' button that resets the conversation history. Persist the current session's chat history to `sessionStorage` so it survives page refreshes within the same browser session. Show a subtle 'Remembering your conversation context' indicator when the mentor references history context.

Massively expand the knowledge base in `frontend/src/hooks/useCyberMentorKnowledge.ts` to cover all six course domains with encyclopedic depth, functioning entirely client-side. The expansion must cover hundreds of topics across: **Frontend** (HTML5 semantics, CSS3/Flexbox/Grid, JavaScript ES6+, React hooks/context/router, TypeScript, Tailwind, responsive design, accessibility, PWA); **Backend** (Node.js/Express, Python/Django/FastAPI, REST/GraphQL, JWT/OAuth, middleware, error handling); **Full Stack** (MERN/MEAN, Docker, CI/CD, Nginx, database migrations, monorepo); **Cyber Security** (OWASP Top 10, XSS/SQLi/CSRF/buffer overflow, penetration testing, Nmap/Metasploit/Burp Suite/Wireshark, cryptography, firewall/IDS/IPS); **Ethical Hacking** (reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, reporting, social engineering, CTF, Kali Linux); **AI Development** (Python for AI, ML fundamentals, neural networks, deep learning with TensorFlow/PyTorch, NLP, LLM architecture, prompt engineering, computer vision, AI ethics, model deployment). Each entry must include: a short summary answer (2–3 sentences), a full structured body with headings/bullets/code blocks following the 7-part response format from REQ-37, an empathetic opening phrase, and a keyword array for scoring.
